element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Most prolific enemy in early game
Screen-clearing moves for primary four characters
Decision points determine subsequent boss encounters

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe zones between combat encounters
Cutscenes at branching path decision points
typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of enemy types with specific strengths/weaknesses
Branching path encounters with unfamiliar boss fights
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based combat with escalating difficulty; multiple branching paths create variable tension arcs; boss encounters punctuate progression with major difficulty spikes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of weak Fnord enemies and basic mechanics; single character choices from four balanced-to-specialized fighters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy type variety increases with distinct strengths/weaknesses; ranged mechanics (Al's bazooka, Orbot's bullets) introduce spacing requirements; branching paths begin affecting boss selection
difficulty_curve.late_game: Super moves become necessary; transformation states complicate enemy patterns; multiple endings based on cumulative path choices; meta-awareness of boss sequences
notable_spikes: Transition to early boss encounters after Fnord waves
Branching path divergence creating unexpected boss matchups
Unlockable characters (Norton, Orbot) as late-game difficulty jump
Final ending sequences with decision-point consequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Early stages feature prolific weak enemy (Fnord) waves punctuated by brief safe zones; teaches rhythm of attack-recovery-attack
Multiple routes through the game with different boss encounters; each path creates unique difficulty and story sequencing
Story-driven levels with literary/cultural references (Stalag 17 - DM labs); environments reinforce narrative context
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stalag 17 (Deutschland Moldavia labs), Musashi Plaza, Snake Plisski street level

notable_level_patterns.description: Previously defeated character-types (other Orbots) reappear as mini-bosses with variable positioning throughout stages
Rick Norton and Orbot transitions from boss encounters to playable characters; unlockable progression tied to narrative beats
